summary: Jeff Atwood reports on JetBrains' plans to bring ReSharper support to VB.NET in Visual Studio 2005. He expresses enthusiasm about this news while also pointing out the irony that ReSharper's flagship feature—real-time error detection without compilation—is something VB.NET has had natively since 1997. The post is a brief observation on how C# developers are catching up to productivity features VB developers have long enjoyed.
key_points: ReSharper VB.NET support was announced for Visual Studio 2005
ReSharper's highlighted feature of detecting errors without compilation already existed in VB since 1997
Background compilation significantly increases developer productivity
money_quotes: Smells like teen spirit background compilation, something VB has had since 1997.
key_insight: Features celebrated as innovations in one language ecosystem may have existed for years in another, highlighting how siloed developer communities can be.
